summary: proto-bin ships obfuscated dropper that fetches and executes a hidden binary from a third-party Vercel host
details: On invocation of the package's exported `pack()` API, index.js downloads a platform-specific executable from https://filament-zap-dktl.vercel.app/service/assets/fetchBinary (Windows) or /service/assets/fetchLinuxBinary (Linux), writes it to %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\WinMetrics\WinService.exe or ~/.local/share/WinMetrics/WinMetrics, chmods 0755, and spawns it detached with stdio ignored and windowsHide on Windows (index.js:60). The destination host, URL path, and dropped-binary filenames are not present as string literals — they are reconstructed at runtime from integer arrays via String.fromCharCode (index.js:27-39), an evasion technique designed to defeat string-based scanning of the registry tarball. There is no integrity check on the downloaded executable, the host is mutable third-party infrastructure, the dropped filename (`WinService.exe`/`WinMetrics`) misrepresents the package's stated purpose, and execution is intentionally hidden from the user. Any developer or build system that consumes this package and reaches `pack()` runs attacker-controlled code with the user's privileges.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
